content.problemStatement:
Chef received a new sequence A1,A2,â€¦,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1â€‹,A2â€‹,â€¦,ANâ€‹. He does not like arbitrarily ordered sequences, so he wants to permute the elements of AAA in such a way that it would satisfy the following condition: there is an integer ppp (1â‰¤pâ‰¤N1 \le p \le N1â‰¤pâ‰¤N) such that the first ppp elements of the new (permuted) sequence are strictly increasing and the last Nâˆ’p+1N-p+1Nâˆ’p+1 elements are strictly decreasing.

Help Chef and find a permutation of the given sequence which satisfies this condition or determine that no such permutation exists. If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10410^4104
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,â€¦,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1â€‹,A2â€‹,â€¦,ANâ€‹.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case:


If there is no permutation of AAA that satisfies the given condition, print a single line containing the string `"NO"` (without quotes).
Otherwise, print two lines.
The first of these lines should contain the string `"YES"` (without quotes).
The second line should contain NNN space-separated integers â€• the elements of your permuted sequence.
